2604.19447.title: 'The Order in the Horse's Heart': A Case Study in LLM-Assisted Stylometry for the Discovery of Biblical Allusion in Modern Literary Fiction
2604.19447.paper_summary: We present a dual-track pipeline for detecting biblical allusions in literary fiction and apply it to the novels of Cormac McCarthy. A bottom-up embedding track uses inverse document frequency to identify rare vocabulary shared with the King James Bible, embeds occurrences in their local context for sense disambiguation, and passes candidate passage pairs through cascaded LLM review. A top-down register track asks an LLM to read McCarthy's prose undirected to any specific biblical passage for comparison, catching allusions not distinguished by word or phrase rarity. Both tracks are cross-validated by a long-context model that holds entire novels alongside the KJV in a single pass, and every finding is checked against published scholarship. Restricting attention to allusions that carry a textual echo--shared phrasing, reworked vocabulary, or transplanted cadence--and distinguishing literary allusions proper from signposted biblical references (similes naming biblical figures, characters overtly citing scripture), the pipeline surfaces 349 allusions across the corpus. Among a target set of 115 previously documented allusions retrieved through human review of the academic literature, the pipeline independently recovers 62 (54% recall), with recall varying by connection type from 30% (transformed imagery) to 80% (register collisions). We contextualise these results with respect to the value-add from LLMs as assistants to mechanical stylometric analyses, and their potential to facilitate the statistical study of intertextuality in massive literary corpora.
2604.19447.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個雙軌管道，用於檢測文學小說中的聖經典故，並將其應用於科馬克·麥卡錫的小說。<lb/>一個自下而上的嵌入軌道使用逆文檔頻率來識別與《聖經詹姆斯版》共享的稀有詞彙，將出現的詞彙嵌入其局部上下文中以進行意義消歧，並通過級聯的LLM審查傳遞候選段落對。<lb/>一個自上而下的登記軌道要求LLM閱讀麥卡錫的散文，而不指向任何特定的聖經段落進行比較，捕捉那些不因詞語或短語的稀有性而被區分的典故。<lb/>這兩個軌道都由一個長上下文模型進行交叉驗證，該模型在單次處理中將整部小說與KJV一起持有，並檢查每一個發現是否符合已發表的學術研究。<lb/>將注意力限制在帶有文本回聲的典故上——共享的措辭、重構的詞彙或移植的韻律——並區分文學典故本身與標示的聖經引用（命名聖經人物的明喻、明確引用經文的角色），該管道在整個語料庫中挖掘出349個典故。<lb/>在通過人類對學術文獻的審查檢索的115個目標典故中，該管道獨立恢復了62個（54%的回憶率），不同連結類型的回憶率從30%（轉化的意象）到80%（登記碰撞）不等。<lb/>我們將這些結果與LLM作為機械風格分析助手的附加價值以及它們促進對龐大文學語料庫中互文性進行統計研究的潛力進行了背景說明。
2604.19447.author: Ewan Cameron et.al.
